arabic: الٓرۚ تِلۡكَ ءَايَٰتُ ٱلۡكِتَٰبِ وَقُرۡءَانٖ مُّبِينٖ
transliteration: Aliflamra tilka ayatu alkitabi waquranin mubeenin
translation: Alif Lam Ra. Those are Verses of the Book and it is a clear Qur’aan (Reader).
notes:
Refer study note 2:1 (Chapter 2) regarding such initial letters at the beginning of some Qur’aanic Chapters.

The suppressors of Truth shall so wish while suffering in Hell.

arabic: رُّبَمَا يَوَدُّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ لَوۡ كَانُواْ مُسۡلِمِينَ
transliteration: Rubama yawaddu allatheena kafaroo law kanoo muslimeena
translation: Those who suppress the Truth shall frequently wish that they had been Muslims (submitters to divine Will).
notes:
The suppressors of Truth shall so wish while suffering in Hell.

Verses 14 to 18 herein above seem to foretell modern man’s forays into outer space and powerful telescopes like the Hubble space telescope, which is taking breathtaking pictures of star constellations and other innumerable wonders of the Universe. These Verses point out to the sad fact that despite Allah facilitating mankind to have this knowledge about the mind-boggling workmanship of His, most men still have no belief in the Creator as He is depicted in the Qur’aan. About a visible flame following a satanic person stealing a secret, we are aware of some ventures into space meeting fiery ends. Maybe, those were examples of astronauts persisting in their atheistic ways despite being witnesses to divine wonders.

arabic: ذَرۡهُمۡ يَأۡكُلُواْ وَيَتَمَتَّعُواْ وَيُلۡهِهِمُ ٱلۡأَمَلُۖ فَسَوۡفَ يَعۡلَمُونَ
transliteration: Tharhum yakuloo wayatamattaAAoo wayulhihimu alamalu fasawfa yaAAlamoona
translation: Leave them free to eat and enjoy, and let desire distract them. They will soon know.
notes: Science does not yet seem to have come to definite conclusions about the formation of mountains. And this word used here appears to suggest that mountains were thrown or bombarded on to the surface of the earth from outer space. Maybe, eons ago, during the formation of the earth, parts from an exploding star impounded the earth and the mountains were formed. Remember that mountains have deep roots inside the earth. Otherwise they should be flattened out because of the tremendous speed at which the earth rotates.

arabic: وَمَآ أَهۡلَكۡنَا مِن قَرۡيَةٍ إِلَّا وَلَهَا كِتَابٞ مَّعۡلُومٞ
transliteration: Wama ahlakna min qaryatin illa walaha kitabun maAAloomun
translation: And never did We destroy any population, but only when it had reached its known destiny.
notes: The light pollen grains from a flower get carried by the wind and then get deposited on the stigma, resulting in fertilization.

arabic: مَّا تَسۡبِقُ مِنۡ أُمَّةٍ أَجَلَهَا وَمَا يَسۡتَـٔۡخِرُونَ
transliteration: Ma tasbiqu min ommatin ajalaha wama yastakhiroona
translation: No people can prepone their end nor can they postpone it.
notes: Mankind is given just temporary (and only apparent) sway and material possessions on earth. Everything then gets returned to divine Dominion.

arabic: وَقَالُواْ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِي نُزِّلَ عَلَيۡهِ ٱلذِّكۡرُ إِنَّكَ لَمَجۡنُونٞ
transliteration: Waqaloo ya ayyuha allathee nuzzila AAalayhi alththikru innaka lamajnoonun
translation: And they say, “O you to whom the Reminder (Qur’aan) has been revealed! You are indeed mad.”
notes: From Verses 26 and 27 above, we learn, in the light of modern-day knowledge: • Human beings are made of matter. • Jinn are made of energy radiating from fire. They cannot therefore normally be seen by human beings. • Jinn were created before humans.

arabic: لَّوۡمَا تَأۡتِينَا بِٱلۡمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ
transliteration: Law ma tateena bialmalaikati in kunta mina alssadiqeena
translation: “Why do you not bring to us the angels if what you claim is true?”
notes: Verse 17:85 informs us that the Rooh is a command of the Lord. The Verse also informs that Man has been given but little knowledge.

arabic: مَا نُنَزِّلُ ٱلۡمَلَـٰٓئِكَةَ إِلَّا بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَمَا كَانُوٓاْ إِذٗا مُّنظَرِينَ
transliteration: Ma nunazzilu almalaikata illa bialhaqqi wama kanoo ithan munthareena
translation: We do not send the angels but only when necessary (to destroy wicked persons, for instance), and then they would not be given any respite.
notes: Refer study notes 2:27 and 2:28 (Chapter 2) in this context.

arabic: إِنَّا نَحۡنُ نَزَّلۡنَا ٱلذِّكۡرَ وَإِنَّا لَهُۥ لَحَٰفِظُونَ
transliteration: Inna nahnu nazzalna alththikra wainna lahu lahafithoona
translation: Indeed, We have sent down the Reminder (Qur’aan) and indeed We are most certainly guardians to it.
notes: In verse 18:50, Iblees has been described as a Jinni.

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ أَرۡسَلۡنَا مِن قَبۡلِكَ فِي شِيَعِ ٱلۡأَوَّلِينَ
transliteration: Walaqad arsalna min qablika fee shiyaAAi alawwaleena
translation: And We did certainly send Messengers, before you, among the earlier generations.
notes: What Lot tried to tell his people is that Allah Almighty has designed mankind to be heterosexual in nature. Their homosexuality was a wicked perversity that Satan induced in them as a futile attempt to defeat the divine plan. Lot’s offer of his daughters here must be read in that context. His people were so steeped in the vice of homosexuality that Lot’s offer served the purpose only of highlighting the perverse behaviour of his people. They were not inclined even to look at the daughters. This perverse tendency in some people is sought to be accepted and legalized by modern societies now! Such modern people may not get annihilated now as Lot’s people were for their sin as they live in mixed societies, but they may not escape the intensely more painful punishment in the Hereafter.

arabic: وَمَا يَأۡتِيهِم مِّن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا كَانُواْ بِهِۦ يَسۡتَهۡزِءُونَ
transliteration: Wama yateehim min rasoolin illa kanoo bihi yastahzioona
translation: And never did a Messenger come to them but they mocked him.
notes: Watch the YouTube video.

arabic: كَذَٰلِكَ نَسۡلُكُهُۥ فِي قُلُوبِ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمِينَ
transliteration: Kathalika naslukuhu fee quloobi almujrimeena
translation: Thus do We make it (making a mockery of a Messenger) enter the hearts of the sinners.
notes: One of the pre-historic peoples about whom some more details are given in Verses 26:176 to 26:191. Shu’aib was the Prophet sent to them.

arabic: لَا يُؤۡمِنُونَ بِهِۦ وَقَدۡ خَلَتۡ سُنَّةُ ٱلۡأَوَّلِينَ
transliteration: La yuminoona bihi waqad khalat sunnatu alawwaleena
translation: They believe not in it (the divine Message brought by the Messenger), just as the earlier peoples, that passed away, did not believe (in the divine Messages brought to them by Messengers sent to them).
notes: I.e., the remains of the dwellings of the people of Lot and of the people of the wood. Refer note 12 above.

arabic: وَلَوۡ فَتَحۡنَا عَلَيۡهِم بَابٗا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ فَظَلُّواْ فِيهِ يَعۡرُجُونَ
transliteration: Walaw fatahna AAalayhim baban mina alssamai fathalloo feehi yaAArujoona
translation: And they won’t believe, even if We open to them a gateway into the sky above, and they keep on ascending thereinto.
notes: From Verse 82 below we learn that these people built their houses in the mountains. Verse 7:74 (Manzil II) informs us that the people of AAad had built their houses in the mountains. It’s likely therefore that the AAad and the dwellers of the Rock were the same pople.

arabic: لَقَالُوٓاْ إِنَّمَا سُكِّرَتۡ أَبۡصَٰرُنَا بَلۡ نَحۡنُ قَوۡمٞ مَّسۡحُورُونَ
transliteration: Laqaloo innama sukkirat absaruna bal nahnu qawmun mashooroona
translation: They would still only say, “Our sights.are dazed. Nay, we are a people bewitched!”
notes: Just as the modern-day Muslims have turned away from the Qur’aan.

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ جَعَلۡنَا فِي ٱلسَّمَآءِ بُرُوجٗا وَزَيَّنَّـٰهَا لِلنَّـٰظِرِينَ
transliteration: Walaqad jaAAalna fee alssamai buroojan wazayyannaha lilnnathireena
translation: And certainly We have made constellations in the skies above and We have made it look beautiful for the beholders.
notes: Refer study note 15 under Verse 11.67 (Manzil II).

arabic: وَحَفِظۡنَٰهَا مِن كُلِّ شَيۡطَٰنٖ رَّجِيمٍ
transliteration: Wahafithnaha min kulli shaytanin rajeemin
translation: And We have guarded it against every accursed Satan.
notes: Human beings are Allah’s representatives on earth. And Allah is Gracious and Merciful. They should therefoe reflect those divine attributes in their deeds here. A human being is not entitled to punish another, just because the latter would not believe in Islam. Allah will deal with such people on the Judgment Day, which is bound to come. Those who are given authority over other people on this earth ought to exercise their authority only in such cases where any inaction on their part could raise law and order problems here.

arabic: إِلَّا مَنِ ٱسۡتَرَقَ ٱلسَّمۡعَ فَأَتۡبَعَهُۥ شِهَابٞ مُّبِينٞ
transliteration: Illa mani istaraqa alssamAAa faatbaAAahu shihabun mubeenun
translation: Except that a visible flame follows the one that manages to steal a celestial secret.
notes:
Verses 14 to 18 herein above seem to foretell modern man’s forays into outer space and powerful telescopes like the Hubble space telescope, which is taking breathtaking pictures of star constellations and other innumerable wonders of the Universe. These Verses point out to the sad fact that despite Allah facilitating mankind to have this knowledge about the mind-boggling workmanship of His, most men still have no belief in the Creator as He is depicted in the Qur’aan. About a visible flame following a satanic person stealing a secret, we are aware of some ventures into space meeting fiery ends. Maybe, those were examples of astronauts persisting in their atheistic ways despite being witnesses to divine wonders.

The word ‘Verses’ is not specifically mentioned in the Arabic text. It is the general interpretation of what ‘seven of the repeated’ means. And the seven Verses that are often recited are obviously those of the opening chapter Al-Fatiha of the Qur’aan. The Shia sect interprets the ‘seven’ to be seven of their Imams. But their claim has no merit (refer Interpretation of Verse 15:87).

arabic: وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ مَدَدۡنَٰهَا وَأَلۡقَيۡنَا فِيهَا رَوَٰسِيَ وَأَنۢبَتۡنَا فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ شَيۡءٖ مَّوۡزُونٖ
transliteration: Waalarda madadnaha waalqayna feeha rawasiya waanbatna feeha min kulli shayin mawzoonin
translation: And We have spread out the earth and cast mountains into it. And We have caused every appropriate thing to grow in it.
notes:
Science does not yet seem to have come to definite conclusions about the formation of mountains. And this word used here appears to suggest that mountains were thrown or bombarded on to the surface of the earth from outer space. Maybe, eons ago, during the formation of the earth, parts from an exploding star impounded the earth and the mountains were formed. Remember that mountains have deep roots inside the earth. Otherwise they should be flattened out because of the tremendous speed at which the earth rotates.

The Qur’aan speaks here about most of mankind in today’s world. Most of the world population today is non-Muslim. They have little regard for the Qur’aan. But the tragic fact is that most of the Muslims too treat the Qur’aan as a thing of no consequence in their lives. They have fallen into the habit of reading it just ritualistically, without trying to understand what they read. And even those who read it with some understanding allow themselves to be led astray by extraneous human- influenced sources and deviate from the plain meanings of the Qur’aanic words. These ‘learned’ people are wont to give the words meanings that suit them, and thus divisions have occurred within those who ‘understand’ the Qur’aan. Then there are those who abide by what a part of the Qur’aan says, but do not abide by another part of it. Some may offer the ritual prayers but may consider fasting for a month too difficult for them.

arabic: وَجَعَلۡنَا لَكُمۡ فِيهَا مَعَٰيِشَ وَمَن لَّسۡتُمۡ لَهُۥ بِرَٰزِقِينَ
transliteration: WajaAAalna lakum feeha maAAayisha waman lastum lahu biraziqeena
translation: And We have created in it means of subsistence for you, and for him whom you do not provide for.
notes: Refer in this context to the Prophet’s distress mentioned in Verse 97 above.

arabic: وَإِن مِّن شَيۡءٍ إِلَّا عِندَنَا خَزَآئِنُهُۥ وَمَا نُنَزِّلُهُۥٓ إِلَّا بِقَدَرٖ مَّعۡلُومٖ
transliteration: Wain min shayin illa AAindana khazainuhu wama nunazziluhu illa biqadarin maAAloomin
translation: And there is nothing of which We do not have treasures with us. And We do not send it down but in a measured quantity.
notes: Waarsalna alrriyaha lawaqiha faanzalna mina alssama-i maan faasqaynakumoohu wama antum lahu bikhazineena

arabic: وَأَرۡسَلۡنَا ٱلرِّيَٰحَ لَوَٰقِحَ فَأَنزَلۡنَا مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءٗ فَأَسۡقَيۡنَٰكُمُوهُ وَمَآ أَنتُمۡ لَهُۥ بِخَٰزِنِينَ
transliteration: Waarsalna alrriyaha lawaqiha faanzalna mina alssamai maan faasqaynakumoohu wama antum lahu bikhazineena
translation: And We send the winds that facilitate pollination , and then send down water from the sky for you to drink. And it is not in your power to store it up.
notes:
The light pollen grains from a flower get carried by the wind and then get deposited on the stigma, resulting in fertilization.

Wa-inna lanahnu nuhyee wanumeetu wanahnu alwarithoona

arabic: وَإِنَّا لَنَحۡنُ نُحۡيِۦ وَنُمِيتُ وَنَحۡنُ ٱلۡوَٰرِثُونَ
transliteration: Wainna lanahnu nuhyee wanumeetu wanahnu alwarithoona
translation: And indeed it is We Who give life and cause death. And We are the heirs .
notes:
Mankind is given just temporary (and only apparent) sway and material possessions on earth. Everything then gets returned to divine Dominion.

Walaqad AAalimna almustaqdimeena minkum walaqad AAalimna almusta/khireena

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ عَلِمۡنَا ٱلۡمُسۡتَقۡدِمِينَ مِنكُمۡ وَلَقَدۡ عَلِمۡنَا ٱلۡمُسۡتَـٔۡخِرِينَ
transliteration: Walaqad AAalimna almustaqdimeena minkum walaqad AAalimna almustakhireena
translation: And We do certainly know those of you who have preceded, and We do certainly know those who would follow.
notes: Wa-inna rabbaka huwa yahshuruhum innahu hakeemun AAaleemun

arabic: وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ يَحۡشُرُهُمۡۚ إِنَّهُۥ حَكِيمٌ عَلِيمٞ
transliteration: Wainna rabbaka huwa yahshuruhum innahu hakeemun AAaleemun
translation: And your Lord will indeed gather them together. He is indeed Wise, Knowledgeable.
notes: Walaqad khalaqna al-insana min salsalin min hama-in masnoonin

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ خَلَقۡنَا ٱلۡإِنسَٰنَ مِن صَلۡصَٰلٖ مِّنۡ حَمَإٖ مَّسۡنُونٖ
transliteration: Walaqad khalaqna alinsana min salsalin min hamain masnoonin
translation: And certainly We created man of clay, of moulded mud.
notes: Waaljanna khalaqnahu min qablu min nari alssamoomi

arabic: وَٱلۡجَآنَّ خَلَقۡنَٰهُ مِن قَبۡلُ مِن نَّارِ ٱلسَّمُومِ
transliteration: Waaljanna khalaqnahu min qablu min nari alssamoomi
translation: And the jinn We created before, of radiating fire.
notes:
From Verses 26 and 27 above, we learn, in the light of modern-day knowledge: • Human beings are made of matter. • Jinn are made of energy radiating from fire. They cannot therefore normally be seen by human beings. • Jinn were created before humans.

Wa-ith qala rabbuka lilmala-ikati innee khaliqun basharan min salsalin min hama-in masnoonin

arabic: وَإِذۡ قَالَ رَبُّكَ لِلۡمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ إِنِّي خَٰلِقُۢ بَشَرٗا مِّن صَلۡصَٰلٖ مِّنۡ حَمَإٖ مَّسۡنُونٖ
transliteration: Waith qala rabbuka lilmalaikati innee khaliqun basharan min salsalin min hamain masnoonin
translation: And your Lord said to the angels, “I am going to create a man of clay, of moulded mud.”
notes: Fa-itha sawwaytuhu wanafakhtu feehi min roohee faqaAAoo lahu sajideena

arabic: فَإِذَا سَوَّيۡتُهُۥ وَنَفَخۡتُ فِيهِ مِن رُّوحِي فَقَعُواْ لَهُۥ سَٰجِدِينَ
transliteration: Faitha sawwaytuhu wanafakhtu feehi min roohee faqaAAoo lahu sajideena
translation: “So when I give him a proper form and breathe into him of My Rooh , get down to your knees prostrating .”
notes:
Verse 17:85 informs us that the Rooh is a command of the Lord. The Verse also informs that Man has been given but little knowledge.

Refer study notes 2:27 and 2:28 (Chapter 2) in this context.

Fasajada almala-ikatu kulluhum ajmaAAoona

arabic: فَسَجَدَ ٱلۡمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ كُلُّهُمۡ أَجۡمَعُونَ
transliteration: Fasajada almalaikatu kulluhum ajmaAAoona
translation: So the angels prostrated – all of them together.
notes: Illa ibleesa aba an yakoona maAAa alssajideena

arabic: إِلَّآ إِبۡلِيسَ أَبَىٰٓ أَن يَكُونَ مَعَ ٱلسَّـٰجِدِينَ
transliteration: Illa ibleesa aba an yakoona maAAa alssajideena
translation: Except Iblees – he refused to be with those who prostrated.
notes:
In verse 18:50, Iblees has been described as a Jinni.

Qala ya ibleesu ma laka alla takoona maAAa alssajideena

arabic: قَالَ يَـٰٓإِبۡلِيسُ مَا لَكَ أَلَّا تَكُونَ مَعَ ٱلسَّـٰجِدِينَ
transliteration: Qala ya ibleesu ma laka alla takoona maAAa alssajideena
translation: Allah asked, “O Iblees! What excuses have you that you are not with those who prostrated?”
notes: Qala lam akun li-asjuda libasharin khalaqtahu min salsalin min hama-in masnoonin

arabic: قَالَ لَمۡ أَكُن لِّأَسۡجُدَ لِبَشَرٍ خَلَقۡتَهُۥ مِن صَلۡصَٰلٖ مِّنۡ حَمَإٖ مَّسۡنُونٖ
transliteration: Qala lam akun liasjuda libasharin khalaqtahu min salsalin min hamain masnoonin
translation: Iblees said, “I am not the one to prostrate to a man whom You have created of clay, of moulded mud.”
notes: Qala faokhruj minha fa-innaka rajeemun

arabic: قَالَ فَٱخۡرُجۡ مِنۡهَا فَإِنَّكَ رَجِيمٞ
transliteration: Qala faokhruj minha fainnaka rajeemun
translation: Allah said, “Then get out of here! You are indeed damned.”
notes: Wa-inna AAalayka allaAAnata ila yawmi alddeeni

arabic: وَإِنَّ عَلَيۡكَ ٱللَّعۡنَةَ إِلَىٰ يَوۡمِ ٱلدِّينِ
transliteration: Wainna AAalayka allaAAnata ila yawmi alddeeni
translation: “And indeed the curse is on you till the Judgment Day.”
notes: Qala rabbi faanthirnee ila yawmi yubAAathoona

arabic: قَالَ رَبِّ فَأَنظِرۡنِيٓ إِلَىٰ يَوۡمِ يُبۡعَثُونَ
transliteration: Qala rabbi faanthirnee ila yawmi yubAAathoona
translation: He said, “My Lord! Then give me respite till the time when they are raised.”
notes: Qala fa-innaka mina almunthareena

arabic: قَالَ فَإِنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلۡمُنظَرِينَ
transliteration: Qala fainnaka mina almunthareena
translation: Allah said, “You are granted the respite”
notes: Ila yawmi alwaqti almaAAloomi

arabic: إِلَىٰ يَوۡمِ ٱلۡوَقۡتِ ٱلۡمَعۡلُومِ
transliteration: Ila yawmi alwaqti almaAAloomi
translation: “For the appointed period.”
notes: Qala rabbi bima aghwaytanee laozayyinanna lahum fee al-ardi walaoghwiyannahum ajmaAAeena

arabic: قَالَ رَبِّ بِمَآ أَغۡوَيۡتَنِي لَأُزَيِّنَنَّ لَهُمۡ فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَلَأُغۡوِيَنَّهُمۡ أَجۡمَعِينَ
transliteration: Qala rabbi bima aghwaytanee laozayyinanna lahum fee alardi walaoghwiyannahum ajmaAAeena
translation: He said, “My Lord! As You have made me deviate from the right way, I will certainly keep them enthralled in the earth, and I will certainly cause them all to deviate”
notes: Illa AAibadaka minhumu almukhlaseena

arabic: إِلَّا عِبَادَكَ مِنۡهُمُ ٱلۡمُخۡلَصِينَ
transliteration: Illa AAibadaka minhumu almukhlaseena
translation: “Except for those among them who worship you devotedly and exclusively.”
notes: Qala hatha siratun AAalayya mustaqeemun

arabic: قَالَ هَٰذَا صِرَٰطٌ عَلَيَّ مُسۡتَقِيمٌ
transliteration: Qala hatha siratun AAalayya mustaqeemun
translation: Allah said, “This is the Path that leads straight to Me.”
notes: Inna AAibadee laysa laka AAalayhim sultanun illa mani ittabaAAaka mina alghaweena

arabic: إِنَّ عِبَادِي لَيۡسَ لَكَ عَلَيۡهِمۡ سُلۡطَٰنٌ إِلَّا مَنِ ٱتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ ٱلۡغَاوِينَ
transliteration: Inna AAibadee laysa laka AAalayhim sultanun illa mani ittabaAAaka mina alghaweena
translation: “Indeed, you shall have no power over My subjects, except those who follow you in deviating from the right way.”
notes: Wa-inna jahannama lamawAAiduhum ajmaAAeena

arabic: وَإِنَّ جَهَنَّمَ لَمَوۡعِدُهُمۡ أَجۡمَعِينَ
transliteration: Wainna jahannama lamawAAiduhum ajmaAAeena
translation: And indeed Hell is the place promised to them all.
notes: Laha sabAAatu abwabin likulli babin minhum juz-on maqsoomun

arabic: لَهَا سَبۡعَةُ أَبۡوَٰبٖ لِّكُلِّ بَابٖ مِّنۡهُمۡ جُزۡءٞ مَّقۡسُومٌ
transliteration: Laha sabAAatu abwabin likulli babin minhum juzon maqsoomun
translation: It has seven gates assigned separately to separate sections of them.
notes: Inna almuttaqeena fee jannatin waAAuyoonin

arabic: إِنَّ ٱلۡمُتَّقِينَ فِي جَنَّـٰتٖ وَعُيُونٍ
transliteration: Inna almuttaqeena fee jannatin waAAuyoonin
translation: And those who fear Allah shall indeed be in gardens and fountains.
notes: Odkhulooha bisalamin amineena

arabic: ٱدۡخُلُوهَا بِسَلَٰمٍ ءَامِنِينَ
transliteration: Odkhulooha bisalamin amineena
translation: They shall enter there in peace and security.
notes: WanazaAAna ma fee sudoorihim min ghillin ikhwanan AAala sururin mutaqabileena

arabic: وَنَزَعۡنَا مَا فِي صُدُورِهِم مِّنۡ غِلٍّ إِخۡوَٰنًا عَلَىٰ سُرُرٖ مُّتَقَٰبِلِينَ
transliteration: WanazaAAna ma fee sudoorihim min ghillin ikhwanan AAala sururin mutaqabileena
translation: And We will clean their hearts of any rancour. They shall be on couches face to face as brethren. 15:48 They shall not have to suffer any fatigue therein, nor shall they be ever ejected from it.
notes:
La yamassuhum feeha nasabun wama hum minha bimukhrajeena 15:48 They shall not have to suffer any fatigue therein, nor shall they be ever ejected from it.

Nabbi/ AAibadee annee ana alghafooru alrraheemu

arabic: لَا يَمَسُّهُمۡ فِيهَا نَصَبٞ وَمَا هُم مِّنۡهَا بِمُخۡرَجِينَ
transliteration: La yamassuhum feeha nasabun wama hum minha bimukhrajeena

arabic: ۞نَبِّئۡ عِبَادِيٓ أَنِّيٓ أَنَا ٱلۡغَفُورُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
transliteration: Nabbi AAibadee annee ana alghafooru alrraheemu
translation: Inform my subjects that I am the Forgiver, the Merciful. 15:50 And that My punishment is the painful one.
notes:
Waanna AAathabee huwa alAAathabu al-aleemu 15:50 And that My punishment is the painful one.

Wanabbi/hum AAan dayfi ibraheema

arabic: وَأَنَّ عَذَابِي هُوَ ٱلۡعَذَابُ ٱلۡأَلِيمُ
transliteration: Wa anna AAathabee huwa alAAathabu alaleemu

arabic: وَنَبِّئۡهُمۡ عَن ضَيۡفِ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَ
transliteration: Wanabbihum AAan dayfi ibraheema
translation: And tell them about Abraham’s guests.
notes: Ith dakhaloo AAalayhi faqaloo salaman qala inna minkum wajiloona

arabic: إِذۡ دَخَلُواْ عَلَيۡهِ فَقَالُواْ سَلَٰمٗا قَالَ إِنَّا مِنكُمۡ وَجِلُونَ
transliteration: Ith dakhaloo AAalayhi faqaloo salaman qala inna minkum wajiloona
translation: When they came to him and greeted, “Peace!” He said, “We are indeed apprehensive of you.”
notes: Qaloo la tawjal inna nubashshiruka bighulamin AAaleemin

arabic: قَالُواْ لَا تَوۡجَلۡ إِنَّا نُبَشِّرُكَ بِغُلَٰمٍ عَلِيمٖ
transliteration: Qaloo la tawjal inna nubashshiruka bighulamin AAaleemin
translation: They said, “Be not afraid! We do indeed give you the good news of a learned son.”
notes: Qala abashshartumoonee AAala an massaniya alkibaru fabima tubashshirooni

arabic: قَالَ أَبَشَّرۡتُمُونِي عَلَىٰٓ أَن مَّسَّنِيَ ٱلۡكِبَرُ فَبِمَ تُبَشِّرُونَ
transliteration: Qala abashshartumoonee AAala an massaniya alkibaru fabima tubashshirooni
translation: He said, “Do you give me the good news when old age has come upon me? What kind of good news is this that you give me?”
notes: Qaloo bashsharnaka bialhaqqi fala takun mina alqaniteena

arabic: قَالُواْ بَشَّرۡنَٰكَ بِٱلۡحَقِّ فَلَا تَكُن مِّنَ ٱلۡقَٰنِطِينَ
transliteration: Qaloo bashsharnaka bialhaqqi fala takun mina alqaniteena
translation: They said, “There is truth in the good news We give you! So be not of those who despair.
notes: Qala waman yaqnatu min rahmati rabbihi illa alddalloona

arabic: قَالَ وَمَن يَقۡنَطُ مِن رَّحۡمَةِ رَبِّهِۦٓ إِلَّا ٱلضَّآلُّونَ
transliteration: Qala waman yaqnatu min rahmati rabbihi illa alddalloona
translation: He said, “And who would despair of the Mercy of his Lord but those who have gone astray?”
notes: Qala fama khatbukum ayyuha almursaloona

arabic: قَالَ فَمَا خَطۡبُكُمۡ أَيُّهَا ٱلۡمُرۡسَلُونَ
transliteration: Qala fama khatbukum ayyuha almursaloona
translation: He said, “What is your mission then, O Messengers?”
notes: Qaloo inna orsilna ila qawmin mujrimeena

arabic: قَالُوٓاْ إِنَّآ أُرۡسِلۡنَآ إِلَىٰ قَوۡمٖ مُّجۡرِمِينَ
transliteration: Qaloo inna orsilna ila qawmin mujrimeena
translation: They said, “We have indeed been sent towards a sinning people.”
notes: Illa ala lootin inna lamunajjoohum ajmaAAeena

arabic: إِلَّآ ءَالَ لُوطٍ إِنَّا لَمُنَجُّوهُمۡ أَجۡمَعِينَ
transliteration: Illa ala lootin inna lamunajjoohum ajmaAAeena
translation: “Excepting Lot's family, all of whom We certainly will save.”
notes: Illa imraatahu qaddarna innaha lamina alghabireena

arabic: إِلَّا ٱمۡرَأَتَهُۥ قَدَّرۡنَآ إِنَّهَا لَمِنَ ٱلۡغَٰبِرِينَ
transliteration: Illa imraatahu qaddarna innaha lamina alghabireena
translation: “But not his wife. We ordained that she shall indeed be of those who remain behind.”
notes: Falamma jaa ala lootin almursaloona

arabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَ ءَالَ لُوطٍ ٱلۡمُرۡسَلُونَ
transliteration: Falamma jaa ala lootin almursaloona
translation: So when the Messengers came to Lot's family,
notes: Qala innakum qawmun munkaroona

arabic: قَالَ إِنَّكُمۡ قَوۡمٞ مُّنكَرُونَ
transliteration: Qala innakum qawmun munkaroona
translation: He said, “You indeed are strangers.”
notes: Qaloo bal ji/naka bima kanoo feehi yamtaroona

arabic: قَالُواْ بَلۡ جِئۡنَٰكَ بِمَا كَانُواْ فِيهِ يَمۡتَرُونَ
transliteration: Qaloo bal jinaka bima kanoo feehi yamtaroona
translation: They said, “But we have come to you with that about which they had doubts.” 15:64 “And we have come to you with the authority, and what we say is most certainly the truth.”
notes:
Waataynaka bialhaqqi wa-inna lasadiqoona 15:64 “And we have come to you with the authority, and what we say is most certainly the truth.”

Faasri bi-ahlika biqitAAin mina allayli waittabiAA adbarahum wala yaltafit minkum ahadun waimdoo haythu tu/maroona

arabic: وَأَتَيۡنَٰكَ بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَإِنَّا لَصَٰدِقُونَ
transliteration: Waataynaka bialhaqqi wainna lasadiqoona

arabic: فَأَسۡرِ بِأَهۡلِكَ بِقِطۡعٖ مِّنَ ٱلَّيۡلِ وَٱتَّبِعۡ أَدۡبَٰرَهُمۡ وَلَا يَلۡتَفِتۡ مِنكُمۡ أَحَدٞ وَٱمۡضُواْ حَيۡثُ تُؤۡمَرُونَ
transliteration: Faasri biahlika biqitAAin mina allayli waittabiAA adbarahum wala yaltafit minkum ahadun waimdoo haythu tumaroona
translation: “So leave this place with members of your family in a part of the night and you yourself follow their rear. And let not any one of you turn around. And proceed whither you are commanded.”
notes: Waqadayna ilayhi thalika al-amra anna dabira haola-i maqtooAAun musbiheena

arabic: وَقَضَيۡنَآ إِلَيۡهِ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلۡأَمۡرَ أَنَّ دَابِرَ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ مَقۡطُوعٞ مُّصۡبِحِينَ
transliteration: Waqadayna ilayhi thalika alamra anna dabira haolai maqtooAAun musbiheena
translation: And We declared to him that decree, that the roots of these (people left behind) shall be cut off by the morning.
notes: Wajaa ahlu almadeenati yastabshiroona

arabic: وَجَآءَ أَهۡلُ ٱلۡمَدِينَةِ يَسۡتَبۡشِرُونَ
transliteration: Wajaa ahlu almadeenati yastabshiroona
translation: And the people of the town came to him rejoicing.
notes: Qala inna haola-i dayfee fala tafdahooni

arabic: قَالَ إِنَّ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ ضَيۡفِي فَلَا تَفۡضَحُونِ
transliteration: Qala inna haolai dayfee fala tafdahooni
translation: He said, “These are my guests! Disgrace me not.”
notes: Waittaqoo Allaha wala tukhzooni

arabic: وَٱتَّقُواْ ٱللَّهَ وَلَا تُخۡزُونِ
transliteration: Waittaqoo Allaha wala tukhzooni
translation: “And fear Allah and do not put me to shame.” 15:70 They said, “Have we not prohibited you from meddling with other people’s matters?”
notes:
Qaloo awa lam nanhaka AAani alAAalameena 15:70 They said, “Have we not prohibited you from meddling with other people’s matters?”

Qala haola-i banatee in kuntum faAAileena

arabic: قَالُوٓاْ أَوَلَمۡ نَنۡهَكَ عَنِ ٱلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
transliteration: Qaloo awalam nanhaka AAani alAAalameena

arabic: قَالَ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ بَنَاتِيٓ إِن كُنتُمۡ فَٰعِلِينَ
transliteration: Qala haolai banatee in kuntum faAAileena
translation: He said, “Here are my daughters, if you have to do it. ”
notes:
What Lot tried to tell his people is that Allah Almighty has designed mankind to be heterosexual in nature. Their homosexuality was a wicked perversity that Satan induced in them as a futile attempt to defeat the divine plan. Lot’s offer of his daughters here must be read in that context. His people were so steeped in the vice of homosexuality that Lot’s offer served the purpose only of highlighting the perverse behaviour of his people. They were not inclined even to look at the daughters. This perverse tendency in some people is sought to be accepted and legalized by modern societies now! Such modern people may not get annihilated now as Lot’s people were for their sin as they live in mixed societies, but they may not escape the intensely more painful punishment in the Hereafter.

LaAAamruka innahum lafee sakratihim yaAAmahoona

arabic: لَعَمۡرُكَ إِنَّهُمۡ لَفِي سَكۡرَتِهِمۡ يَعۡمَهُونَ
transliteration: LaAAamruka innahum lafee sakratihim yaAAmahoona
translation: By your life! They were wandering blindly in their intoxication.
notes: Faakhathat-humu alssayhatu mushriqeena

arabic: فَأَخَذَتۡهُمُ ٱلصَّيۡحَةُ مُشۡرِقِينَ
transliteration: Faakhathathumu alssayhatu mushriqeena
translation: Then, at sunrise, the terribly rumbling sound struck them.
notes: FajaAAalna AAaliyaha safilaha waamtarna AAalayhim hijaratan min sijjeelin

arabic: فَجَعَلۡنَا عَٰلِيَهَا سَافِلَهَا وَأَمۡطَرۡنَا عَلَيۡهِمۡ حِجَارَةٗ مِّن سِجِّيلٍ
transliteration: FajaAAalna AAaliyaha safilaha waamtarna AAalayhim hijaratan min sijjeelin
translation: We turned the place upside down, and rained stones of baked clay upon the people.
notes: Inna fee thalika laayatin lilmutawassimeena

arabic: إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَأٓيَٰتٖ لِّلۡمُتَوَسِّمِينَ
transliteration: Inna fee thalika laayatin lilmutawassimeena
translation: In that indeed are signs for those with insight.
notes: Wa-innaha labisabeelin muqeemin

arabic: وَإِنَّهَا لَبِسَبِيلٖ مُّقِيمٍ
transliteration: Wainnaha labisabeelin muqeemin
translation: And indeed it is located on an established road.
notes:
Watch the YouTube video.

Inna fee thalika laayatan lilmu/mineena

arabic: إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَأٓيَةٗ لِّلۡمُؤۡمِنِينَ
transliteration: Inna fee thalika laayatan lilmumineena
translation: Indeed there is a sign in that for the believers.
notes: Wa-in kana as-habu al-aykati lathalimeena

arabic: وَإِن كَانَ أَصۡحَٰبُ ٱلۡأَيۡكَةِ لَظَٰلِمِينَ
transliteration: Wain kana ashabu alaykati lathalimeena
translation: And the dwellers of the wood too were most wicked.
notes:
One of the pre-historic peoples about whom some more details are given in Verses 26:176 to 26:191. Shu’aib was the Prophet sent to them.

Faintaqamna minhum wa-innahuma labi-imamin mubeenin

arabic: فَٱنتَقَمۡنَا مِنۡهُمۡ وَإِنَّهُمَا لَبِإِمَامٖ مُّبِينٖ
transliteration: Faintaqamna minhum wainnahuma labiimamin mubeenin
translation: So We avenged them, and they are both , indeed, on an open road.
notes:
I.e., the remains of the dwellings of the people of Lot and of the people of the wood. Refer note 12 above.

Walaqad kaththaba as-habu alhijri almursaleena

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ كَذَّبَ أَصۡحَٰبُ ٱلۡحِجۡرِ ٱلۡمُرۡسَلِينَ
transliteration: Walaqad kaththaba ashabu alhijri almursaleena
translation: And the dwellers of the Rock did certainly reject the Messengers.
notes:
From Verse 82 below we learn that these people built their houses in the mountains. Verse 7:74 (Manzil II) informs us that the people of AAad had built their houses in the mountains. It’s likely therefore that the AAad and the dwellers of the Rock were the same pople.

Waataynahum ayatina fakanoo AAanha muAArideena

arabic: وَءَاتَيۡنَٰهُمۡ ءَايَٰتِنَا فَكَانُواْ عَنۡهَا مُعۡرِضِينَ
transliteration: Waataynahum ayatina fakanoo AAanha muAArideena
translation: And We gave them Our Verses/signs, but they turned away from them.
notes:
Just as the modern-day Muslims have turned away from the Qur’aan.

Wakanoo yanhitoona mina aljibali buyootan amineena

arabic: وَكَانُواْ يَنۡحِتُونَ مِنَ ٱلۡجِبَالِ بُيُوتًا ءَامِنِينَ
transliteration: Wakanoo yanhitoona mina aljibali buyootan amineena
translation: And they were wont to hew houses in the mountains for security.
notes: Faakhathat-humu alssayhatu musbiheena

arabic: فَأَخَذَتۡهُمُ ٱلصَّيۡحَةُ مُصۡبِحِينَ
transliteration: Faakhathathumu alssayhatu musbiheena
translation: So the terribly rumbling sound seized them in the morning.
notes:
Refer study note 15 under Verse 11.67 (Manzil II).

Fama aghna AAanhum ma kanoo yaksiboona

arabic: فَمَآ أَغۡنَىٰ عَنۡهُم مَّا كَانُواْ يَكۡسِبُونَ
transliteration: Fama aghna AAanhum ma kanoo yaksiboona
translation: And what they earned availed them not.
notes: Wama khalaqna alssamawati waal-arda wama baynahuma illa bialhaqqi wa-inna alssaAAata laatiyatun faisfahi alssafha aljameela

arabic: وَمَا خَلَقۡنَا ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَآ إِلَّا بِٱلۡحَقِّۗ وَإِنَّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ لَأٓتِيَةٞۖ فَٱصۡفَحِ ٱلصَّفۡحَ ٱلۡجَمِيلَ
transliteration: Wama khalaqna alssamawati waalarda wama baynahuma illa bialhaqqi wainna alssaAAata laatiyatun faisfahi alssafha aljameela
translation: And We did not create the heavens and the earth and what is between them, but by due authority. And the Hour is most surely going to come. So fogive things – those that are forgivable.
notes:
Human beings are Allah’s representatives on earth. And Allah is Gracious and Merciful. They should therefoe reflect those divine attributes in their deeds here. A human being is not entitled to punish another, just because the latter would not believe in Islam. Allah will deal with such people on the Judgment Day, which is bound to come. Those who are given authority over other people on this earth ought to exercise their authority only in such cases where any inaction on their part could raise law and order problems here.

Inna rabbaka huwa alkhallaqu alAAaleemu

arabic: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ ٱلۡخَلَّـٰقُ ٱلۡعَلِيمُ
transliteration: Inna rabbaka huwa alkhallaqu alAAaleemu
translation: Your Lord is indeed the Knowledgeable Creator of all things.
notes: Walaqad ataynaka sabAAan mina almathanee waalqur-ana alAAatheema

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ ءَاتَيۡنَٰكَ سَبۡعٗا مِّنَ ٱلۡمَثَانِي وَٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ ٱلۡعَظِيمَ
transliteration: Walaqad ataynaka sabAAan mina almathanee waalqurana alAAatheema
translation: And certainly We have given you seven of the repeated Verses and the Glorious Qur’aan.
notes:
The word ‘Verses’ is not specifically mentioned in the Arabic text. It is the general interpretation of what ‘seven of the repeated’ means. And the seven Verses that are often recited are obviously those of the opening chapter Al-Fatiha of the Qur’aan. The Shia sect interprets the ‘seven’ to be seven of their Imams. But their claim has no merit (refer Interpretation of Verse 15:87).

La tamuddanna AAaynayka ila ma mattaAAna bihi azwajan minhum wala tahzan AAalayhim waikhfid janahaka lilmu/mineena

arabic: لَا تَمُدَّنَّ عَيۡنَيۡكَ إِلَىٰ مَا مَتَّعۡنَا بِهِۦٓ أَزۡوَٰجٗا مِّنۡهُمۡ وَلَا تَحۡزَنۡ عَلَيۡهِمۡ وَٱخۡفِضۡ جَنَاحَكَ لِلۡمُؤۡمِنِينَ
transliteration: La tamuddanna AAaynayka ila ma mattaAAna bihi azwajan minhum wala tahzan AAalayhim waikhfid janahaka lilmumineena
translation: Pine not for that which We have given some of them to enjoy, nor grieve for them. And be kind to the believers.
notes: Waqul innee ana alnnatheeru almubeenu

arabic: وَقُلۡ إِنِّيٓ أَنَا ٱلنَّذِيرُ ٱلۡمُبِينُ
transliteration: Waqul innee ana alnnatheeru almubeenu
translation: And say, “I am indeed the plain warner.”
notes: Kama anzalna AAala almuqtasimeena

arabic: كَمَآ أَنزَلۡنَا عَلَى ٱلۡمُقۡتَسِمِينَ
transliteration: Kama anzalna AAala almuqtasimeena
translation: It is as though what We have sent down is on those who make divisions therein.
notes: Allatheena jaAAaloo alqur-ana AAideena

arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ جَعَلُواْ ٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ عِضِينَ
transliteration: Allatheena jaAAaloo alqurana AAideena
translation: Those that tear the Qur’aan into shreds.
notes:
The Qur’aan speaks here about most of mankind in today’s world. Most of the world population today is non-Muslim. They have little regard for the Qur’aan. But the tragic fact is that most of the Muslims too treat the Qur’aan as a thing of no consequence in their lives. They have fallen into the habit of reading it just ritualistically, without trying to understand what they read. And even those who read it with some understanding allow themselves to be led astray by extraneous human- influenced sources and deviate from the plain meanings of the Qur’aanic words. These ‘learned’ people are wont to give the words meanings that suit them, and thus divisions have occurred within those who ‘understand’ the Qur’aan. Then there are those who abide by what a part of the Qur’aan says, but do not abide by another part of it. Some may offer the ritual prayers but may consider fasting for a month too difficult for them.

Fawarabbika lanas-alannahum ajmaAAeena

arabic: فَوَرَبِّكَ لَنَسۡـَٔلَنَّهُمۡ أَجۡمَعِينَ
transliteration: Fawarabbika lanasalannahum ajmaAAeena
translation: And, by your Lord, We shall certainly question them all
notes: AAamma kanoo yaAAmaloona

arabic: عَمَّا كَانُواْ يَعۡمَلُونَ
transliteration: AAamma kanoo yaAAmaloona
translation: As to what they did!
notes: FaisdaAA bima tu/maru waaAArid AAani almushrikeena

arabic: فَٱصۡدَعۡ بِمَا تُؤۡمَرُ وَأَعۡرِضۡ عَنِ ٱلۡمُشۡرِكِينَ
transliteration: FaisdaAA bima tumaru waaAArid AAani almushrikeena
translation: Declare then openly what you are commanded with and turn away from the polytheists.
notes: Inna kafaynaka almustahzi-eena

arabic: إِنَّا كَفَيۡنَٰكَ ٱلۡمُسۡتَهۡزِءِينَ
transliteration: Inna kafaynaka almustahzieena
translation: We shall certainly provide you sufficient protection against those who scoff.
notes: Allatheena yajAAaloona maAAa Allahi ilahan akhara fasawfa yaAAlamoona

arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يَجۡعَلُونَ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا ءَاخَرَۚ فَسَوۡفَ يَعۡلَمُونَ
transliteration: Allatheena yajAAaloona maAAa Allahi ilahan akhara fasawfa yaAAlamoona
translation: Those who worship others besides Allah. They shall soon know their folly!
notes: Walaqad naAAlamu annaka yadeequ sadruka bima yaqooloona

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ نَعۡلَمُ أَنَّكَ يَضِيقُ صَدۡرُكَ بِمَا يَقُولُونَ
transliteration: Walaqad naAAlamu annaka yadeequ sadruka bima yaqooloona
translation: And We do know that your (Prophet’s) heart is distressed at what they say.
notes: Fasabbih bihamdi rabbika wakun mina alssajideena

arabic: فَسَبِّحۡ بِحَمۡدِ رَبِّكَ وَكُن مِّنَ ٱلسَّـٰجِدِينَ
transliteration: Fasabbih bihamdi rabbika wakun mina alssajideena
translation: Hymn then the praise of your Lord and be of those who prostrate to Him.
notes: WaoAAbud rabbaka hatta ya/tiyaka alyaqeenu

arabic: وَٱعۡبُدۡ رَبَّكَ حَتَّىٰ يَأۡتِيَكَ ٱلۡيَقِينُ
transliteration: WaoAAbud rabbaka hatta yatiyaka alyaqeenu
translation: And worship your Lord until there comes to you that certain composure.
notes: Refer in this context to the Prophet’s distress mentioned in Verse 97 above.
